rulings.Case Title: PARKVIEW EDGE PROPERTIES, LLC VS. FLEUR FOSTER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday December 15, 2016, line 1. DEFENDANT FLEUR FOSTER, SHEIBA BOISSIERE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION DENIED. Moving party failed to comply with CRC 3.1350(b). See Notice of Motion and Separate Statement. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: PARKVIEW EDGE PROPERTIES, LLC VS. FLEUR FOSTER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday December 15, 2016, line 1. PLAINTIFF PARKVIEW EDGE PROPERTIES, LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication DENIED. Plaintiff's Motion for Summary Adjudication is DENIED. Plaintiff's Separate Statement and the improperly filed Amended Separate Statement fail to comply with CRC 3.1350(b). Plaintiff's Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED there is a triable issue of fact regarding existence of the tenancy. The Court also notes that Plaintiff's Separate Statement is devoid of any facts regarding non-existence of tenancy. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: EVERETT ADAMS ET AL VS. DIPAK PATEL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant'S Motion To Compel Plaintiffs To Respond To Requests For Admissions, Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, And Docment Requests, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiffs And Their Counsel Of Record, Jointly And Severally
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday December 15, 2016, line 2. Defendant'S Motion To Compel Plaintiffs To Respond To Requests For Admissions, Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, And Docment Requests, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiffs And Their Counsel Of Record, Jointly And Severally GRANTED. Plaintiffs must provide complete verified responses to Requests for Admission, From Interrogatories and Special Interrogatories, and Request for Production within 10 days of notice of entry of order. Sanctions granted in the amount of $7,860. The Court notes that (1) the issue of deceased Plaintiffs discussed at December 5, 2016 has not yet been addressed; (2) Plaintiff filed additional opposition papers in violation of December 6, 2016 order; (3) Plaintiff failed to provide courtesy copy of the moving papers in violation of December 6, 2016. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES MA VS. CATHERINE HERRERA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday December 15, 2016, line 6. DEFENDANT CATHERINE HERRERA DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege the capacity of the individual who entered into the rental agreement with Defendant (paragraph 6(b)(4)). Plaintiff cites no authority for the proposition that a tenant of the former owner is new owner's "predecessor in interest." Plaintiff shall also attach all agreements "regarding the premises" including the lease agreement between the owner and Manuel Corona. =(501/REQ)
